So one cargo ship gets sideways in a ditch and suddenly the entire global economy is having a meltdown. Because apparently we built our whole civilization on the assumption that boats would always go straight.
Oil companies see one blocked canal and immediately jack up prices by four dollars a barrel. It's almost like they were just waiting for any excuse to squeeze more money out of everyone.
Europeans are hoarding toilet paper again because some guy in Egypt can't parallel park a container ship. We really learned nothing from 2020, did we.
German car factories shut down because they can't get computer chips from Asia for a week and a half. Remember when cars just had engines and didn't need to be smartphones on wheels?
British hipsters are facing an oat milk shortage and treating it like the apocalypse has arrived. God forbid they have to drink regular milk like some kind of peasant.

Meanwhile, some poor delivery driver in Middle America is pulling double shifts because Karen's decorative throw pillows are stuck somewhere in the Mediterranean. The real hero of this whole disaster if you ask me.

And here we are, a full month later, paying TWO EXTRA DOLLARS for a burrito because avocados had to take a slight detour around Africa. TWO DOLLARS! For what, the inconvenience of sailing around a continent that humans have been navigating for literally thousands of years?!
